response: Phone imagine a season where Samford played twelve games with no ties. Phone their victories were twice the number of their losses, creating a clear distinction. Phone the team scored twenty-eight points per game in each win, showing strong performance. Phone they managed fourteen points per game in losses, reflecting a different level of play. Phone calculating the total points requires knowing how many games were won and lost. Phone if losses are x, then wins are two x, leading to three x equals twelve games. Phone solving for x gives four losses and eight wins. Phone multiplying eight wins by twenty-eight points gives two hundred twenty-four points. Phone multiplying four losses by fourteen points gives fifty-six points. Phone adding two hundred twenty-four and fifty-six gives two hundred eighty total points for the season.
